title: Lower Trust Closeout Audit
goal: Produce a compact checked-in audit for issue #324 that reconciles lower-trust issue closeouts from the no-active-plan implementation run.
Map each audited closed issue to fully satisfied with evidence, bounded slice satisfied with parent intent still open, covered by open follow-up, or needs reopening.
Preserve #324's constraint that existing roadmap lanes must not be closed until the audit makes the evidence state explicit.
non_goals: Do not close existing roadmap lanes as part of this audit.
Do not reopen issues unless the checked evidence clearly shows an uncovered dangling intent.
Do not implement the follow-up issues found by the audit in this slice.

intent.outcome: Make the lower-trust closeout state reviewable and machine-consumable before treating prior lanes as satisfied.
intent.constraints: Use live GitHub issue state and checked-in planning evidence; do not rely on chat memory alone; keep the artifact compact enough for future agents to consume.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest review artifact shape that records issue classification, evidence pointers, lane impact, and follow-up routing.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the audit finds closed issues with material uncovered intent that cannot be represented by an existing open follow-up.

execution.next_step: Validate the audit artifact, close #324, then continue the machine-first lane with #323/#325.
execution.proof: A checked-in review artifact maps the audited issue set to explicit satisfaction categories, references evidence/follow-ups, and leaves existing roadmap lanes open.

invariants: Existing roadmap lanes remain open until their satisfaction state is separately promoted or closed.
Audit claims cite live issues, commits, plans, reviews, or explicit open follow-ups.
The active planning record remains singular and valid while the slice is in progress.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: machine-first planning state and closeout hygiene

required_continuation.activation trigger: continue with #323/#325 after #324 establishes the lower-trust evidence map

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Future lane closeout can start from explicit evidence categories instead of reconstructing trust state from chat and commits.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: archive cleanup hardening (#323), reconciliation view/tooling (#325), and any parent-lane implementation work outside the audit.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: No audited item needs immediate reopening, but many issue closures are bounded-slice evidence rather than lane-closure evidence.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: lower-trust closeout audit artifact created with per-issue classification and lane impact
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: #323 or #325 in the same roadmap lane

intent_interpretation.literal request: execute planned work lane by lane, dogfooding the package fully
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: start with the first roadmap lane's first slice and use the planning package as the operational source of truth
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: promote #324 into an active execplan and create the lower-trust closeout audit artifact
intent_interpretation.review guidance: verify the artifact covers #324's issue set and does not prematurely close existing lanes

execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before changing package code or GitHub issue state beyond closing #324 after proof
execution_bounds.stop before touching: roadmap lane closure, unrelated execplans, or implementation follow-ups

stop_conditions.stop when: the audit is checked in, validated, and #324 can honestly close as an audit artifact milestone
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: the audit identifies a closed issue that needs reopening and no existing follow-up covers the remaining intent
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: work becomes implementation of the audited follow-up issues
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: planning checks fail or the audit cannot cite evidence

context_budget.live working set: #324 body, issue states for the audited set, roadmap lanes, recent planning artifacts and commits
context_budget.recoverable later: full archived plan history outside cited evidence
context_budget.externalize before shift: classification table, follow-up mapping, and lane impact
context_budget.pre-work config pull: agentic-workspace preflight --format json; agentic-workspace summary --format json
context_budget.tiny resumability note: audit #324 first; do not close lanes; use live issue states and checked-in evidence
context_budget.context-shift triggers: after issue evidence collection or before committing

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: create a trustworthy closeout audit for lower-trust prior work
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: do not close roadmap lanes; use live GitHub issue state; keep artifact compact and machine-readable
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: exact grouping, evidence pointer format, and whether #324 can close after artifact proof
delegated_judgment.escalate when: material dangling work is found without an open follow-up or clear reopen target

active_milestone.scope: Create one compact review artifact for lower-trust closed issue evidence state.

immediate_next_action: Run validation commands, then close #324 and continue with #323/#325.
invariants: No existing roadmap lane is closed by this slice.
Open follow-ups remain visible in planning state.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: A lane cannot be considered closed from this no-active-plan run until the audit categorizes its issues and follow-ups.
open_questions_to_close: Which closed issues are fully satisfied?
Which closed issues only completed a bounded slice?
Which closed issues are covered by open follow-ups?
completion_criteria: The review artifact includes the issue set, classifications, evidence, follow-up mapping, and lane impact.
The active execplan records proof and honest continuation.
#324 can close without closing any existing roadmap lane.

execution_run.what happened: Promoted #324 into an active execplan, gathered live GitHub issue state and local planning/commit evidence, and created a checked-in review artifact classifying each audited closeout.
execution_run.changed surfaces: planning active state, #324 execplan, lower-trust closeout audit review artifact
execution_run.validations run: uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; git diff --check
execution_run.result for continuation: #324 can close after validation; no existing roadmap lane should close from this audit alone.

finished_run_review.config compliance: preflight used before raw planning edits
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low after per-issue classifications and lane impact were checked in
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: close #324 after validation, keep roadmap lanes open

proof_report.validation proof: `uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json`; `uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py`; `git diff --check`
proof_report.proof achieved now: A checked-in audit artifact maps the lower-trust closeouts to explicit trust categories and records open follow-up coverage.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: audit lower-trust roadmap lane closeouts before closing existing lanes
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The review artifact classifies #292-#316 plus #261/#279/#278/#306/#275 and adjacent #287/#288/#290, with evidence/follow-up/lane-impact fields; follow-ups #323 and #325 are closed.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Agentic Workspace should make agent work restartable, reviewable, and grounded in structured local surfaces rather than chat-only state.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: prefer compact machine-readable evidence over broad prose closeout
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can future agents decide lane continuation or closure from checked-in evidence without reconstructing the no-active-plan run?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The audit answers #324's requested reconciliation question, follow-ups #323 and #325 are closed, and live planning state now carries only #230.
closure_check.reopen trigger: reopen or create follow-up if the audit finds closed issue intent not covered by open work

execution_summary.validation confirmed: summary, planning surface check, and diff whitespace check passed

drift_log: 2026-04-26: Active execplan created from roadmap lane `machine-first-planning-state` suggested first slice for #324.
2026-04-26: Added lower-trust closeout audit artifact with per-issue classifications and lane impacts.
2026-04-26: Validation passed and the active TODO row was removed, leaving the completed plan as durable proof for #324.
